body_txt: A Sub-Trope of Power Trio , this is when an ensemble of three characters represents a dynamic of three different time periods: the past, the present, and the future. Past : Represents what was or what has been . If age is a factor, is usually the oldest (representing someone/something whose prime is in the past). Positive traits associated with the archetype include wisdom and prudence (their experience usually leads to them not being rash). Negative traits usually associated with the archetype are usually focused around cynicism and laziness (a negative aspect of their experience). Corresponds to Crone in The Hecate Sisters , Wife in The Three Faces of Eve , Prophet in The Three Faces of Adam , Elder in Three Successful Generations and Knave in Knight, Knave, and Squire . Present : Represents what is . If age is a factor, will likely be in-between (representing someone/something that lies between the past and future). Positive traits usually associated with the archetype include liveliness and adaptability (to represent the present's ever-changing nature). Negative traits usually associated with the archetype are usually focused around capriciousness and/or short-sightedness (i.e. rarely thinking beyond the present). Corresponds to Mother in The Hecate Sisters , Seductress in The Three Faces of Eve (representing the liveliness and beauty of the present), Lord in The Three Faces of Adam , Adult in Three Successful Generations , and Knight in Knight, Knave, and Squire . Future : Represents what will be or what could be . If age is a factor, will likely be the youngest of the three (representing someone/something that will reach their/its prime in the future). Positive traits usually associated with the archetype include optimism and industriousness (to contrast the past's cynicism and laziness). Negative traits associated with the archetype include recklessness and impatience. Corresponds to Maiden in The Hecate Sisters , Child in The Three Faces of Eve (representing the optimism and naivety of youth) Hunter in The Three Faces of Adam , Youth in Three Successful Generations , and Squire in Knight, Knave, and Squire . Sometimes, the roles of Past and Future may be switched with each other, with Past now representing the past times when someone/something was still young/new, while the Future represents what will happen in someone's/something's future. If this happens, the traits and associations also get switched. Heavily overlaps with Yet Another Christmas Carol , since the original novel by Charles Dickens , as well as numerous adaptations, has this dynamic with the Ghosts of Christmas Past, Present, and Yet To Come.
